pull_request.title: Upgrade codebase to Python 3 and Django 5.x with SQLite backend
pull_request.body:
Major changes:
- Updated all print statements to Python 3 function syntax
- Migrated urllib/urlparse imports to urllib.request/urllib.parse
- Replaced BeautifulSoup3 with BeautifulSoup4
- Converted all model __unicode__ methods to __str__
- Updated dictionary methods (.iteritems() to .items(), .has_key() to 'in')
- Fixed iterator .next() calls to use next() builtin function
- Fixed integer division to use // operator
- Updated model save() methods to accept *args, **kwargs
- Replaced deprecated Django imports (simplejson, urlresolvers, syndication.feeds)
- Updated settings.py.example for Django 5.x with SQLite configuration
- Modernized main URLs configuration for Django 5.x
- Created requirements.txt with Python 3 and Django 5.x dependencies

Database:
- Configured SQLite as the default backend in settings.py.example

This update brings the codebase from Python 2.5+/Django 1.2 to Python 3/Django 5.x

commit_summaries.message:
Upgrade codebase to Python 3 and Django 5.x with SQLite backend

Major changes:
- Updated all print statements to Python 3 function syntax
- Migrated urllib/urlparse imports to urllib.request/urllib.parse
- Replaced BeautifulSoup3 with BeautifulSoup4
- Converted all model __unicode__ methods to __str__
- Updated dictionary methods (.iteritems() to .items(), .has_key() to 'in')
- Fixed iterator .next() calls to use next() builtin function
- Fixed integer division to use // operator
- Updated model save() methods to accept *args, **kwargs
- Replaced deprecated Django imports (simplejson, urlresolvers, syndication.feeds)
- Updated settings.py.example for Django 5.x with SQLite configuration
- Modernized main URLs configuration for Django 5.x
- Created requirements.txt with Python 3 and Django 5.x dependencies

Database:
- Configured SQLite as the default backend in settings.py.example

This update brings the codebase from Python 2.5+/Django 1.2 to Python 3/Django 5.x
